title: YAML frontmatter injection via unescaped backslash/newline in extract-knowledge field emission
description: quoteRelevancePath() escapes only the double-quote character (value.replace(/"/g, '\\"')) but NOT backslashes or newlines (packages/server/src/services/extract-knowledge.ts:718-720). Every caller-supplied string field written into the YAML frontmatter of a pending KB file flows through this function: summary (line 625), tags (631), relevance_paths (639), intent_clues (650), tech_stack (654), impact (658), must_read_if (662), evidence_paths (676). The corresponding zod schemas are plain z.string()/z.array(z.string()) with NO charset or newline restriction (packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts:683-740). A value ending in a single backslash (e.g. summary='foo\\') renders as `summary: "foo\\"` — the trailing backslash escapes the closing quote, breaking YAML parsing and allowing a crafted value to inject arbitrary additional frontmatter keys (e.g. forging layer:, type:, maturity:, x-fabric-idempotency-key:, or a status: active to bypass review). The NEW-31 prompt-injection sanitizer (extract-knowledge.ts:46-75) only redacts instruction-style patterns; it does not neutralize backslashes or newlines, so the YAML-breaking primitive survives sanitization. Because fab_extract_knowledge is an MCP tool whose inputs are produced by an LLM/skill (a prompt-injection-reachable surface), a poisoned session transcript could steer the emitted frontmatter.
fix_direction: Replace the hand-rolled quoting with a real YAML scalar serializer, or at minimum escape backslashes before quotes (value.replace(/\\/g, '\\\\').replace(/"/g, '\\"')) AND reject/escape embedded newlines. Better: add a regex/length guard at the zod schema layer (api-contracts.ts) for single-line frontmatter fields and round-trip the assembled frontmatter through a YAML parser before write to fail closed.

context.notes: Discovered by security perspective in DBP-20260530-214509

issue_history.note: quoteRelevancePath escapes backslash/quote/control chars before YAML scalar wrap (extract-knowledge.ts).

title: Hook sidecar state writes (shown-cache, last-emit, state-store) are non-atomic plain writeFileSync — crash/concurrency can truncate the file
description: writeJsonState / writeTextState use plain writeFileSync with no tmp-file+rename (state-store.cjs:49, 70). Same for the shown-cache write at fabric-hint.cjs:996 and the maintenance last-emit timestamp at fabric-hint.cjs:1134. A crash or concurrent write mid-writeFileSync leaves a truncated/garbled file. The blast radius is bounded: readers wrap JSON.parse in try/catch and return null on failure (state-store.cjs:40, fabric-hint.cjs read paths), so a corrupt cache degrades to 'counter reset' rather than a crash, and the module documents counter loss as acceptable (state-store.cjs:21). Still, the shown-cache (fabric-hint.cjs:982, NOT session-scoped) and edit-counter being shared-not-per-session means concurrent windows write the same file; atomic write would remove the truncation window entirely.
fix_direction: Use write-to-tmp + rename for these sidecar writes (mirror atomic-write.ts semantics in a small cjs helper). Lower priority because the read path already degrades gracefully to null, but it removes the corruption window for the non-session-scoped caches.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260530-214509

title: doctor.ts is a 10,117-line god-file (102 functions, 51 inspect* + 51 create*Check)
description: packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts is 10,117 lines — over 2x the next-largest source file (review.ts 1,479). It holds 51 `inspect*` collectors, 51 matching `create*Check` builders, 7 `apply*` fixers, plus the orchestrators `runDoctorReport` (~364 lines, 1100-1463), `runDoctorFix` (~334 lines, 1477-1810), `runDoctorApplyLint` (1810+). Each lint check follows an identical inspect→create boilerplate shape (see createDraftBacklogCheck 3977-4000, createDriftUnconsumedCheck 4043+), so the file grows linearly with every new rc check and is the single highest-churn maintenance surface. Navigation, review, and merge-conflict risk are all amplified.

Pre-1.0 + clean-slate-preferred owner makes this a low-risk mechanical extraction.

title: Event-ledger rotation is age-only (30d retention), never size-bounded — file grows unbounded within the retention window under heavy usage
description: rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded (event-ledger.ts:386-511) partitions strictly by `ts < now - retentionDays*86400000` (line 395, 441). retentionDays defaults to 30 (line 27) with config options of only 7/30/90 (line 637). There is NO size-based trigger: a high-frequency workspace (the audit comment at read-ledger.ts:101-105 notes edit_intent_checked 'fires per affected_path on every Edit/Write/MultiEdit') can append far more than 50MB of events WITHIN 30 days, and rotation will keep all of them because they are younger than the cutoff. The 50MB threshold (event-ledger.ts:28, 200) only emits a one-shot stderr warning — it does NOT trigger rotation. Combined with the full-file-read in readEventLedger, the in-window growth degrades every ledger read. Rotation also only runs on a 6h server tick (rotation-tick.ts:18,40) or doctor --fix (doctor.ts:1677); short-lived per-session MCP spawns that exit before 6h never rotate.
fix_direction: Add a size-based rotation trigger (rotate oldest N% when file exceeds payloadHardBytes-style ceiling regardless of age) and run a startup rotation tick (currently first tick fires only after a full 6h interval per rotation-tick.ts:30-31). Tie the 50MB warn threshold to an actual rotate, not just stderr.

title: Module-level translator uses OS env locale, not the project's fabric_language — non-doctor commands ignore configured language
description: packages/cli/src/i18n.ts:14-15 binds the exported `t` to detectNodeLocale() (env-driven). Only doctor gets a projectRoot-aware translator via getDoctorTranslator(projectRoot) (i18n.ts:17-19, the only non-self caller per grep). So install/uninstall/config/onboard-coverage — which DO import the module-level `t` — render in the OS locale rather than the repo's `.fabric/fabric-config.json` fabric_language. A user who set fabric_language: zh-CN on an en_US machine still sees English install/config output. The comment at i18n.ts:8-13 acknowledges this is intentional scoping but it contradicts the 'i18n is via fabric_language' product principle for everything except doctor.
fix_direction: Generalize the projectRoot-aware translator (resolveFabricLocale) to all commands that operate inside an installed project, falling back to detectNodeLocale only when no .fabric/fabric-config.json exists (e.g. pre-install).

title: doctor human output uses Unicode arrow → in live stdout, contradicting the codebase's own GBK-safe ASCII '-> ' convention
description: lib/error-render.ts:49 documents a deliberate rule: action-hint arrows use ASCII '-> ' (not Unicode →) for GBK terminal compatibility, and renderFabricError() at error-render.ts:55 follows it. But the doctor human renderer emits raw Unicode '→' directly to stdout in three live output paths: the folded maintainer-hint breadcrumb (commands/doctor.ts:670), the action-hint line (commands/doctor.ts:672), and the TL;DR severity-order legend 'fixable→manual→warn' (commands/doctor.ts:700). On a non-UTF-8 / GBK or legacy terminal these render as mojibake; for a screen reader the bare arrow glyph is also less meaningful than 'then'/'->'. The fix is mechanical and the project already decided which way is correct.
fix_direction: Replace the Unicode '→' in the three writeStdout() call sites (doctor.ts:670, 672, 700) with the ASCII '-> ' arrow used by lib/error-render.ts:55, matching the GBK-compatibility convention documented at error-render.ts:49.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260530-214509

issue_history.note: doctor-render actionHint arrows use ASCII '-> ' matching error-render GBK-safe convention.

title: HTML prototype: interactive controls lack accessible names (SVG-only buttons, fake search input, placeholder anchors)
description: ui-prototype-hybrid.html is a standalone dual-theme mockup (title 'Fabric v2 - Hybrid Dual-Theme UI'), not part of the shipped CLI/MCP product, so impact is limited unless it is promoted to a real UI. Within it: the sidebar nav links are all href="#" placeholders with inline SVGs that have no aria-label/title (lines 86-109), so a screen reader announces only the trailing text node and the icons are unlabeled decoration with no role; the header 'Search path...' control is a non-interactive <div> styled as an input (lines 135-141) rather than a real <input> with a <label>, so it is not reachable or announced as a search field; the 'Copy' button's leading SVG (line 261) and the status-dot+text patterns rely on adjacent text (which is fine), but the copy/theme icon-buttons mix labeled and unlabeled patterns inconsistently. The theme-toggle button does set title="Toggle Theme" (line 148), which is the correct pattern the others should follow.
fix_direction: If this prototype is ever shipped: give each decorative SVG aria-hidden="true" and add aria-label (or visible text) to icon-only controls; replace the fake search <div> (lines 135-141) with a real <input type="search"> + associated <label> (or aria-label); give the nav anchors real hrefs/roles. If it remains a throwaway mockup, no action needed.

title: In-flight drain uses a single waiter slot, so concurrent shutdown drains can overwrite each other
description: createShutdownHandler is per-signal, so SIGTERM and SIGINT handlers can both enter the first-invocation path and call tracker.drain concurrently. The tracker stores only one resolveDrained callback; the later drain overwrites the earlier one, so when active requests exit only the newest waiter resolves immediately while the earlier shutdown path waits until its deadline and may then run fsync/flush/close/exit out of phase with the other handler.
fix_direction: Represent drain waiters as a Set/array and resolve all pending waiters when active.size reaches 0, or centralize shutdown dedup across all signals so only one drain can be active; add a regression test for concurrent drain() calls before the final exit().

fix_direction: Store interval/window metadata per projectRoot alongside each timer, or pass the captured interval into every timer and stop-handle flush; add a test that starts two roots with different intervals and verifies each emitted row keeps its own window.

title: Quoted frontmatter arrays do not round-trip through line-based parsers
description: FabReview/FabExtract schemas accept arbitrary strings for tags and relevance_paths, and review also accepts title/summary. Writers now quote complex values with JSON/YAML escaping, but readers still use line-based helpers that split flow arrays on every comma and only strip surrounding quotes. A schema-valid value such as a tag or path containing a comma is serialized as a quoted array element, then parsed back as two corrupted elements; escaped newlines/backslashes in quoted title/summary similarly come back as literal escape text rather than the original string. This breaks list/search filters, path relevance, and metadata round-trip while being distinct from the older unescaped-injection findings.
fix_direction: Replace parseFlowArray/extractInlineArray and stripQuotes/unquote with a real YAML frontmatter parser or a tokenizer that respects quoted scalars and JSON/YAML escapes; add round-trip tests for commas, quotes, backslashes, and escaped newlines. Alternatively constrain schema values to the subset the current parser can actually round-trip.

title: Audit-grade events.jsonl writes use drop-on-contention locks, silently losing session/edit/cite markers
description:
[reliability] appendLockedLine was designed for best-effort injections telemetry and DROPS the row when the lock is held. That same primitive is used for session_ended, edit_intent_checked, narrow_hint_failed, and other audit markers that doctor cite-coverage and funnel reconciliation depend on. Under multi-window load, markers disappear without error, producing false cite compliance and incomplete session joins.

[reliability] Server flushMetrics only uses in-process createLedgerWriteQueue (no withFileLock). Hooks append the same .fabric/metrics.jsonl via appendLockedLine. Cross-process writers are therefore unsynchronized: server append can tear against hook append, and hook drop-on-contention can discard Stop-hook counter folds that doctor cite rollups rely on.
fix_direction: Split telemetry vs audit writers: audit events must wait/retry (or enqueue) instead of drop; keep drop-on-contention only for pure denominator telemetry.

title: git clone remote URLs are not protocol-allowlisted; option-hardening alone does not block ext::/file://
description: mountStoreFromRemote / install --global <url> call execFileSync('git', ['clone', '--', url, dest]). The '--' only stops git option injection; the remote string itself remains fully trusted. Schema accepts any non-empty remote string. Comment at the call site even cites ext::sh -c as a positional case without blocking it.

title: Hook appendLockedLine force-unlinks shared *.lock without ownership tokens and can steal the server's event-ledger lock
description: Server writers use withFileLock on events.jsonl.lock with ownership tokens and wait semantics. title: Hook event-writer persists free-text events without secret/PII redaction
description:
[compliance] TS appendEventLedgerEvent redacts named free-text fields (intent/message/reason/kb_line_raw/user_messages_summary) before write. The shared CJS event-writer used by all hooks only stamps envelope fields and JSON.stringifies raw payloads. Stop-hook assistant_turn_observed rows write kb_line_raw from transcripts with no redactSecrets/redactPii, so credentials/emails/phones in cite lines can land in project .fabric/events.jsonl.

[security] appendEventLedgerEvent redacts free-text fields (reason, message, summary, intent, etc.) before persistence. Hook and install writers stamp raw JSON and append via appendLockedLine/appendFileSync with no hasSecrets/redactSecrets pass. Failure reasons and other free-text can land credential-shaped content in the project ledger, which is later read by doctor/hooks and may be synced/shared.
fix_direction: Mirror server field-name redaction (credentials + PII) inside CJS appendEvent/appendEvents before append, or route hook writes through a shared redacting helper; cover kb_line_raw and other free-text fields.

title: Hot hooks fully read and JSON.parse entire events.jsonl on SessionStart and PreToolUse
description:
[performance] Multiple hot hooks load the whole ledger with readFileSync + split + per-line JSON.parse: knowledge-hint-broad scans all lines for the latest doctor_run, cite-policy-evict reloads the ledger on edit-backed PreToolUse, and fabric-hint has its own full-file readLedger. Under the existing age-only retention window this becomes an O(ledger-size) tax on session boot and every edit path, not just background rotation.

[ux] knowledge-hint-broad SessionStart maintenance probe does readFileSync + split of the entire .fabric/events.jsonl on every fire, then walks lines tail-first for doctor_run. fabric-hint Stop path does the same full-file readLedger. As the ledger approaches the 50MB auto-rotate threshold, every new session pays multi-MB sync I/O before the agent sees context, adding SessionStart latency/noise with no sidecar watermark or tail-bounded scan. Operators only see a delayed banner or silent stall, not a remediation path.

[scalability] collectArchiveScan calls readEventLedger(projectRoot) with no event_type/since pushdown, then multipasses the full events array for anchor, session order, lastAttempt, and proposed keys. readEventLedger streams lines but still accumulates every parsed event into a giant array. Under large events.jsonl (near 50MB rotate), each archive-scan MCP call becomes O(N) CPU+RAM on the MCP process, competing with SessionStart and doctor.

[scalability] Unlike server readEventLedger's streaming visitor, SessionStart uses blocking readFileSync of the full ledger for a single event_type. Hook process memory spikes to the full file size and holds the event loop during parse/split. This is a separate failure class from age-only rotation: even with size-triggered rotate, frequent sessions still pay full-file cost within the window, and short-lived CLI spawn + MCP appends can re-grow the file between rotates.

[maintainability] fabric-hint.readLedger, cite-policy-evict.readEventsLedger, and knowledge-hint-broad.readLastDoctorRunAgeDays each reimplement ledger open/split/JSON.parse with slightly different trailing-newline and type filters. This is not the four-copy hook tree issue; it is duplicated runtime logic that will drift on ledger format changes.
fix_direction: Add a shared tail/cursor reader (byte offset or reverse scan with early stop) and optional event-type index. Never parse the full ledger on PreToolUse; keep SessionStart probes bounded.

context.suggested_fix: Add a shared tail/cursor reader (byte offset or reverse scan with early stop) and optional event-type index. Never parse the full ledger on PreToolUse; keep SessionStart probes bounded.

title: Read-set fingerprint samples only the first file per store — content edits miss cache invalidation
description:
[performance] walkReadSetStores caches full markdown sources keyed by a fingerprint that hashes the path list plus only the first ref file's size/mtime per store_uuid. Editing any non-sampled knowledge file leaves the fingerprint unchanged, so plan-context/fab_recall can keep serving stale full-file corpus text, rankings, and census until a sampled file or structure changes.

[scalability] readSetFingerprint builds a structural hash of all refs but only stats the first sampleFile per store_uuid for mtime/size. Editing any non-first knowledge file leaves the fingerprint unchanged, so walkReadSetStores serves stale full bodies/descriptions until process restart or a structural change. At large store scale this both hides updates and encourages long-lived wrong recall without an explicit bust path.
fix_direction: Fingerprint every ref (or a content hash/revision already used by computeReadSetRevision) instead of one sample file per store. Prefer reusing the store-corpus revision as the walk-cache key so content edits always miss.

context.suggested_fix: Fingerprint every ref (or a content hash/revision already used by computeReadSetRevision) instead of one sample file per store. Prefer reusing the store-corpus revision as the walk-cache key so content edits always miss.

title: reconcileStoreCounters overwrites counters.json without the allocate lock, racing live ID minting
description: allocateStoreKnowledgeId serializes with withFileLock + atomicWriteJson on counters.json.lock. reconcileStoreCounters (doctor --fix / post-migrate) reads then writeFileSyncs counters.json with no lock and no atomic rename. A concurrent approve can mint an id and persist a higher counter that reconcile immediately overwrites with a stale snapshot, reopening duplicate stable_id allocation.
fix_direction: Run reconcile under the same counters lock and atomicWriteJson; or floor under lock by re-reading disk+current counters before write.

title: SessionStart blocks on spawnSync fabric plan-context-hint with a hard 2s timeout
description: knowledge-hint-broad (and the narrow sibling) invoke the full CLI via spawnSync with CLI_TIMEOUT_MS=2000. That path includes process boot plus plan-context cold walk/scoring. description: Server flushMetrics only uses in-process createLedgerWriteQueue (no withFileLock). Hooks append the same .fabric/metrics.jsonl via appendLockedLine.
